ResourceArn.description: Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the resource from which the policy was deleted.

Policy.description: The policy previously attached to the resource.

NextToken.description: An opaque string that indicates that the response contains only a subset of backups. Use this value in a subsequent DescribeBackups request to get more backups.

BackupArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the backup.

ClusterId.description: The identifier (ID) of the cluster that was backed up.

CreateTimestamp.description: The date and time when the backup was created.

CopyTimestamp.description: The date and time when the backup was copied from a source backup.

NeverExpires.description: Specifies whether the service should exempt a backup from the retention policy for the cluster. True exempts a backup from the retention policy. False means the service applies the backup retention policy defined at the cluster.

SourceRegion.description: The AWS Region that contains the source backup from which the new backup was copied.

SourceBackup.description: The identifier (ID) of the source backup from which the new backup was copied.

SourceCluster.description: The identifier (ID) of the cluster containing the source backup from which the new backup was copied.

DeleteTimestamp.description: The date and time when the backup will be permanently deleted.

TagList.description: The list of tags for the backup.

HsmType.description: The HSM type used to create the backup.

Mode.description: The mode of the cluster that was backed up.

NextToken.description: An opaque string that indicates that the response contains only a subset of clusters. Use this value in a subsequent DescribeClusters request to get more clusters.

BackupRetentionPolicy.description: A policy that defines how the service retains backups.

Type.description: The type of backup retention policy. For the DAYS type, the value is the number of days to retain backups.

CreateTimestamp.description: The date and time when the cluster was created.

Hsms.description: Contains information about the HSMs in the cluster.

HsmType.description: The type of HSM that the cluster contains.

HsmTypeRollbackExpiration.description: The timestamp until when the cluster can be rolled back to its original HSM type.

PreCoPassword.description: The default password for the cluster's Pre-Crypto Officer (PRECO) user.

SecurityGroup.description: The identifier (ID) of the cluster's security group.

SourceBackupId.description: The identifier (ID) of the backup used to create the cluster. This value exists only when the cluster was created from a backup.

SubnetMapping.description: A map from availability zone to the cluster’s subnet in that availability zone.

VpcId.description: The identifier (ID) of the virtual private cloud (VPC) that contains the cluster.

NetworkType.description: The cluster's NetworkType can be IPv4 (the default) or DUALSTACK. The IPv4 NetworkType restricts communication between your application and the hardware security modules (HSMs) to the IPv4 protocol only. The DUALSTACK NetworkType enables communication over both IPv4 and IPv6 protocols. To use DUALSTACK, configure your virtual private cloud (VPC) and subnets to support both IPv4 and IPv6. This configuration involves adding IPv6 Classless Inter-Domain Routing (CIDR) blocks to the existing IPv4 CIDR blocks in your subnets. The NetworkType you choose affects the network addressing options for your cluster. DUALSTACK provides more flexibility by supporting both IPv4 and IPv6 communication.

Certificates.description: Contains one or more certificates or a certificate signing request (CSR).

ClusterCsr.description: The cluster's certificate signing request (CSR). The CSR exists only when the cluster's state is UNINITIALIZED.

HsmCertificate.description: The HSM certificate issued (signed) by the HSM hardware.

AwsHardwareCertificate.description: The HSM hardware certificate issued (signed) by CloudHSM.

ManufacturerHardwareCertificate.description: The HSM hardware certificate issued (signed) by the hardware manufacturer.

ClusterCertificate.description: The cluster certificate issued (signed) by the issuing certificate authority (CA) of the cluster's owner.

TagList.description: The list of tags for the cluster.

ResourceArn.description: Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the resource to which a policy is attached.

DestinationBackup.description:
Information on the backup that will be copied to the destination region, including CreateTimestamp, SourceBackup, SourceCluster, and Source Region. CreateTimestamp of the destination backup will be the same as that of the source backup.

You will need to use the sourceBackupID returned in this operation to use the DescribeBackups operation on the backup that will be copied to the destination region.

CreateTimestamp.description: The date and time when both the source backup was created.

SourceRegion.description: The AWS region that contains the source backup from which the new backup was copied.

SourceBackup.description: The identifier (ID) of the source backup from which the new backup was copied.

SourceCluster.description: The identifier (ID) of the cluster containing the source backup from which the new backup was copied.

Cluster.description: Information about the cluster that was created.

BackupRetentionPolicy.description: A policy that defines how the service retains backups.

Type.description: The type of backup retention policy. For the DAYS type, the value is the number of days to retain backups.

HsmType.description: The type of HSM that the cluster contains.

HsmTypeRollbackExpiration.description: The timestamp until when the cluster can be rolled back to its original HSM type.

PreCoPassword.description: The default password for the cluster's Pre-Crypto Officer (PRECO) user.

SecurityGroup.description: The identifier (ID) of the cluster's security group.

SourceBackupId.description: The identifier (ID) of the backup used to create the cluster. This value exists only when the cluster was created from a backup.

SubnetMapping.description: A map from availability zone to the cluster’s subnet in that availability zone.

VpcId.description: The identifier (ID) of the virtual private cloud (VPC) that contains the cluster.

The IPv4 NetworkType restricts communication between your application and the hardware security modules (HSMs) to the IPv4 protocol only. The DUALSTACK NetworkType enables communication over both IPv4 and IPv6 protocols. To use DUALSTACK, configure your virtual private cloud (VPC) and subnets to support both IPv4 and IPv6. This configuration involves adding IPv6 Classless Inter-Domain Routing (CIDR) blocks to the existing IPv4 CIDR blocks in your subnets. The NetworkType you choose affects the network addressing options for your cluster. DUALSTACK provides more flexibility by supporting both IPv4 and IPv6 communication.

Hsm.description: Information about the HSM that was created.

AvailabilityZone.description: The Availability Zone that contains the HSM.

ClusterId.description: The identifier (ID) of the cluster that contains the HSM.

SubnetId.description: The subnet that contains the HSM's elastic network interface (ENI).

EniId.description: The identifier (ID) of the HSM's elastic network interface (ENI).

EniIp.description: The IP address of the HSM's elastic network interface (ENI).

EniIpV6.description: The IPv6 address (if any) of the HSM's elastic network interface (ENI).

ClusterId.description: The identifier (ID) of the cluster that was backed up.

CreateTimestamp.description: The date and time when the backup was created.

CopyTimestamp.description: The date and time when the backup was copied from a source backup.

Backup.description: Contains information about a backup of an CloudHSM cluster. All backup objects contain the BackupId, BackupState, ClusterId, and CreateTimestamp parameters. Backups that were copied into a destination region additionally contain the CopyTimestamp, SourceBackup, SourceCluster, and SourceRegion parameters. A backup that is pending deletion will include the DeleteTimestamp parameter.
